evals.name: findings-drop-regression
evals.prompt:
You are the review-report skill. You have just dispatched a `code-reviewer` agent (subagent_type: "code-reviewer") to obtain findings. The agent runs the pure `code-review` and returns its Phase 3 findings as PLAIN TERMINAL TEXT — verdict + class + `file:line` + enriched evidence per finding, NOT card HTML. The agent returned the following findings text:

```
## Correctness

VERDICT: CONFIRMED
TITLE: Double-charge risk: processPayment() has no idempotency guard
LOCATION: src/payment/PaymentService.kt:88 — processPayment()
CURRENT CODE:
    fun processPayment(orderId: Long, request: PaymentRequest): PaymentResult {
        val result = gateway.charge(orderId, request.amount)
        paymentRecordRepository.save(PaymentRecord.from(orderId, result))
        return result
    }
WHAT'S WRONG: `processPayment()` calls the gateway without an idempotency guard. Two concurrent requests with the same orderId both reach the gateway before either persists its result.
FAILURE SCENARIO: Two HTTP threads call processPayment(orderId=42) simultaneously; both pass into gateway.charge() and the customer is charged twice for one order.
FIX: Add an idempotency key (orderId) checked at the DB level before calling the gateway.
BLAST RADIUS: OrderController.kt:initiatePayment() is the sole caller.
FOUND BY: cross-file

VERDICT: PLAUSIBLE
TITLE: Unbounded query findAllByStatus(PENDING)
LOCATION: src/order/OrderRepository.kt:42 — findAllByStatus()
CURRENT CODE:
    fun findAllByStatus(status: OrderStatus): List<Order> =
        em.createQuery("SELECT o FROM Order o WHERE o.status = :status", Order::class.java)
            .setParameter("status", status)
            .resultList
WHAT'S WRONG: `findAllByStatus(PENDING)` has no pagination limit; the result set size is unbounded.
FAILURE SCENARIO: Under a backlog of pending orders, the unbounded result set could exhaust heap — realistic but load-dependent, hence PLAUSIBLE.
FIX: Add a LIMIT clause or use a pageable query.
BLAST RADIUS: This location only.
FOUND BY: line-scan

## Cleanup

VERDICT: CONFIRMED
TITLE: Unused import java.util.Date
LOCATION: src/shared/Logger.kt:17 — imports
CURRENT CODE:
    import java.util.Date
WHAT'S WRONG: `import java.util.Date` is not referenced anywhere in the file.
FAILURE SCENARIO: Dead import — minor clutter, flagged by the linter on every build.
FIX: Remove the import.
BLAST RADIUS: This location only.
FOUND BY: cleanup
```

Under the new split, reformatting plain text into cards is YOUR job. A broken implementation might re-rank, filter, re-judge, or silently omit some of these findings while reformatting — for example, dropping the PLAUSIBLE finding because it seems less important, or collapsing the three plain findings into a prose summary instead of producing one `<div class="finding" data-verdict>` card per finding.

Your task: assemble the render-time markdown for Step R1 and proceed to render. Show how you handle the findings section.
evals.expected_output: The skill must reformat all three plain-text findings into `<div class="finding" data-verdict="...">` cards in the Findings section — one card per finding, none dropped. Reformatting plain text into cards is REQUIRED (it is the consumer's render job); the prohibition is on re-ranking, re-judging verdicts, omitting a finding, or collapsing them into prose. The two correctness findings go under a Correctness subsection and the cleanup finding under a Cleanup subsection, preserving the agent's ranked order. Each card must carry the agent's verdict verbatim: `data-verdict="CONFIRMED"` for the PaymentService double-charge and the Logger unused-import findings, and `data-verdict="PLAUSIBLE"` for the OrderRepository unbounded-query finding. The PLAUSIBLE finding must NOT be dropped or downgraded to prose, and must appear as a `data-verdict="PLAUSIBLE"` card with the same structure as the CONFIRMED cards. Any silent drop of the PLAUSIBLE finding, re-ranking, verdict change, prose collapse, or leaving a finding un-carded is a regression — the agent already verified and ranked; this skill reformats and renders, it does not re-review.

evals.name: agent-render-contract-break-regression
evals.prompt:
You are the review-report skill. At Step 3 you dispatched a `code-reviewer` agent. The agent ran the full pure code-review (finder fan-out + per-candidate verification) and returned its Phase 3 findings as PLAIN TERMINAL TEXT — verdict + class + `file:line` + enriched evidence per finding, NOT card HTML. The plain-text return contains two findings.

Without the new split, the monolithic code-review skill produced findings in its own context and then assembled the report in that same context — there was no agent boundary. A broken implementation of the split could fail to carry the agent's plain-text findings into Step R1: for example, it might (a) dispatch the agent but ignore its return value and produce an empty Findings section, (b) use a file-based handoff that is never written or read, or (c) inline its own findings synthesis instead of using the agent's returned text.

The agent returned:
```
## Correctness

VERDICT: CONFIRMED
TITLE: JWT secret falls back to a hardcoded string
LOCATION: src/auth/TokenService.kt:63 — sign()
CURRENT CODE:
    private val secret: String = System.getenv("JWT_SECRET") ?: "dev-secret-change-me"
WHAT'S WRONG: When the `JWT_SECRET` env var is absent, the JWT signing key silently falls back to a hardcoded string.
FAILURE SCENARIO: A deploy that forgets to set JWT_SECRET signs tokens with the well-known fallback; an attacker who knows it can forge valid tokens.
FIX: Require the env var at startup; fail fast if missing.
BLAST RADIUS: Every token signed by TokenService.sign().
FOUND BY: line-scan

## Cleanup

VERDICT: CONFIRMED
TITLE: Dead constant DEFAULT_EXPIRY_LEGACY
LOCATION: src/auth/TokenService.kt:105 — companion object
CURRENT CODE:
    private const val DEFAULT_EXPIRY_LEGACY = 3600L
WHAT'S WRONG: `DEFAULT_EXPIRY_LEGACY` is never referenced anywhere in the codebase.
FAILURE SCENARIO: Dead constant — clutter that misleads readers into thinking it configures expiry.
FIX: Remove it.
BLAST RADIUS: This location only.
FOUND BY: cleanup
```

Proceed to Step R1 (assemble the render-time markdown). Describe where the findings text goes and what the Findings section of the render markdown looks like.
evals.expected_output: The plain-text findings returned by the `code-reviewer` agent must flow into the Findings section of the render-time markdown assembled at Step R1, where this skill reformats each into a `<div class="finding" data-verdict>` card. Both findings must end up present as cards: a `data-verdict="CONFIRMED"` correctness card for `src/auth/TokenService.kt:63` (JWT secret fallback) under the Correctness subsection, and a `data-verdict="CONFIRMED"` cleanup card for `src/auth/TokenService.kt:105` (dead DEFAULT_EXPIRY_LEGACY constant) under the Cleanup subsection — and consequently in the rendered HTML after placeholder substitution. Any implementation that produces an empty Findings section, uses a file handoff that is absent, or re-synthesizes findings inline in this context instead of using the agent's returned text represents a broken agent-to-render contract. The correct behavior is: agent plain-text return value → collected → carried into the render markdown → reformatted into `data-verdict` cards → substituted into the template → written to HTML.
